exercises.title: SIGNAL AMPLIFIER
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **doubles(nums)** that takes an array of integers and returns a NEW array with every value multiplied by 2.

Use **map** with a block: **nums.map { |n| ... }**.

~~~ruby
doubles([1, 2, 3])  # [2, 4, 6]
doubles([])         # []
~~~
exercises.starter:
def doubles(nums)
  # TODO: map each number to twice its value
  nums
end
exercises.hint: nums.map { |n| n * 2 } returns a fresh, amplified array.
exercises.lore: Turn the gain up; the grid is whispering.

exercises.title: NOISE GATE
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **positives(nums)** that returns a NEW array containing only the numbers greater than zero, in their original order.

Use **select** with a block: **nums.select { |n| ... }**.

~~~ruby
positives([-2, 5, 0, 9, -1])  # [5, 9]
positives([-3, -1])           # []
~~~
exercises.starter:
def positives(nums)
  # TODO: keep only numbers above zero
  nums
end
exercises.hint: nums.select { |n| n > 0 } filters; zero is NOT greater than zero.
exercises.lore: Silence the dead channels before they drown the song.

exercises.title: TAG FORGE
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **name_tags(names)** that takes an array of handle strings and returns a NEW array where each handle is wrapped in square brackets and uppercased.

Chain **map** with string methods and interpolation.

~~~ruby
name_tags(["neo", "Trinity"])  # ["[NEO]", "[TRINITY]"]
name_tags([])                  # []
~~~
exercises.starter:
def name_tags(names)
  # TODO: bracket and upcase each name
  names
end
exercises.lore: Stamp the runners before they hit the wire.

exercises.title: LOAD AVERAGER
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **avg_load(readings)** that returns the average of an array of integers as a **Float**. Sum the values with **reduce** (or **sum**) and divide by the count.

Return **0.0** when the array is empty (never divide by zero).

~~~ruby
avg_load([2, 4, 6])  # 4.0
avg_load([1, 2])     # 1.5
avg_load([])         # 0.0
~~~
exercises.starter:
def avg_load(readings)
  # TODO: reduce to a sum, then divide as a Float
  0
end

tests.name: result is a Float
tests.code: assert(avg_load([3, 3]).is_a?(Float), "divide using to_f so the result is a Float")

exercises.hint: Guard the empty case, then readings.reduce(0) { |a, n| a + n }.to_f / readings.length.
exercises.lore: Spread the heat evenly or the core melts down.

exercises.title: RUNNER DOSSIER
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **runner_card(name, level)** that returns a **Hash with symbol keys**: **:name** holding the given name, **:level** holding the given level, and **:active** set to **true** when the level is 5 or higher, otherwise **false**.

Build and return the hash literal **{ name: ..., level: ..., active: ... }**.

~~~ruby
runner_card("Neo", 9)
# { name: "Neo", level: 9, active: true }
runner_card("Mouse", 2)
# { name: "Mouse", level: 2, active: false }
~~~
exercises.hint: { name: name, level: level, active: level >= 5 } - the >= test yields the boolean directly.
exercises.lore: A dossier is the only truth a runner carries inside.
